title: containers.conf: `containers.env` doesn't work with `podman exec`
body:
### Issue Description

With `~/.config/containers/containers.conf` containing the lines below, `podman exec` doesn't copy the `$TERM` environment variable.

```toml
[containers]
env = ["TERM", {append=true}]
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Put the lines above in `~/.config/containers/containers.conf`
2. Start a container: `TERM=initial podman run -d --name term-test debian:testing sleep 3600`
3. Run something else in the same container, using a different `$TERM`: `TERM=other podman exec term-test env | grep TERM`

### Describe the results you received

Step 3 above printed:
```
TERM=initial
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
TERM=other
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Somewhat related, since the problem I'm trying to solve is incorrect `$TERM` when I run `podman exec -ti`: #25683

I did look to see if this has been changed since version 5.4.0, but I didn't see anything that looked relevant.

comments.body:
I don't think this is a bug. It makes sense that we do not use the containers.conf env setting here as the exec process also uses  the envs from the main container process.

So if you have a env in the config file set but then run the podman run --env to overwrite then the following podman exec must use the env from the container not use the wrong one from the config.

Gotcha, I can see how that would make sense for some (most?) env variables. I don't think it's the right behavior for `TERM`, `COLORTERM`, and maybe a few others, but after thinking about your point I don't think changing the behavior makes sense.

Could this be documented in containers.conf's man page then so other people don't wonder why their env variables aren't being copied?

Yeah I think TERM is indeed a special case where I agree it is unwanted as this is driven by the client side, it makes sense to consider the alternative behaviours for it like you proposed in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25683

Documentation is always good, if you are able please file a PR against containers/common where the man page source is to fix it.

> Documentation is always good, if you are able please file a PR against containers/common where the man page source is to fix it.

Sure, done.
